  • Production or service delivery capacity.
  • Need for technical support.
  • Job creation potential.
  • Socio-economic impact.
  • Entrepreneur motivation.
  • Ability to benefit from mentoring and coaching.

The evaluation aims to identify businesses with strong potential for sustainable growth and regional impact.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must prepare a complete application package containing:

  • Updated Curriculum Vitae (CV).
  • Valid identification document.
  • Company or business profile.
  • Simplified business plan or business model presentation.
  • Financial projections.
  • Company registration documents (where applicable).
  • Any additional supporting documents demonstrating business performance.

All documents must be combined into one PDF file before submission.

Selection Process

The selection process consists of several stages:

  1. Submission of applications.
  2. Administrative verification.
  3. Technical evaluation.
  4. Shortlisting of top applications.
  5. Additional interviews or verification (if required).
  6. Final selection of beneficiaries.
  7. Enrollment into the entrepreneurship support programme.

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

How to Apply

Applicants must send their completed application by email to the address designated for their country.

The required email subject is:

“EYWEP Program Application – Existing Businesses”

Applications should be submitted to the following country-specific email addresses:

  • Benin: benin.eywep@cdjscedeao.org
  • Côte d’Ivoire: cotedivoire.eywep@cdjscedeao.org
  • The Gambia: gambia.eywep@cdjscedeao.org
  • Ghana: ghana.eywep@cdjscedeao.org
  • Guinea-Bissau: guineabissau.eywep@cdjscedeao.org
  • Senegal: senegal.eywep@cdjscedeao.org
  • Sierra Leone: sierraleone.eywep@cdjscedeao.org

Applicants should ensure all required documents are attached as a single PDF before submission.

Important Information

Applicants should note the following:

  • Participation is completely free.
  • Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
  • Late applications will not be accepted.
  • Incomplete applications will not be reviewed.
  • The programme is intended for entrepreneurs with existing businesses rather than business ideas.

For additional enquiries, applicants may contact:

cdjs@ecowas.int

Application Deadline

Deadline: 16 July 2026 at 23:59 GMT

Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications well before the closing date to avoid technical or submission issues.

Why You Should Apply

The ECOWAS Youth Entrepreneurship Programme represents one of the most comprehensive regional entrepreneurship support initiatives available to young business owners in West Africa.

Beyond financial grants, participants gain access to expert mentorship, technical assistance, business management training, investor networks, and regional visibility that can significantly strengthen long-term business growth.

For entrepreneurs operating in sectors such as ICT, renewable energy, agriculture, and pharmaceuticals, the programme offers an excellent opportunity to scale operations, improve competitiveness, create employment, and contribute to regional economic development.

Click HERE to learn more and to apply to the ECOWAS Youth Entrepreneurship Programme (EYEP) 2026.

Conclusion

The ECOWAS Youth Entrepreneurship Programme (EYEP) 2026 provides a unique platform for ambitious young entrepreneurs to strengthen their businesses through grant funding, capacity building, mentoring, and strategic partnerships.

With grants of up to USD 15,000, personalized coaching, networking opportunities, and access to technical and financial partners, the programme equips young entrepreneurs with the resources needed to grow sustainable enterprises and drive economic transformation across West Africa.

Eligible entrepreneurs from Benin, Côte d’Ivoire, The Gambia, Ghana, Guinea-Bissau, Senegal, and Sierra Leone are encouraged to prepare their application documents early and submit complete applications before 16 July 2026 (23:59 GMT).

Discover more global African, Women, and Grant opportunities on OFY.

Reference: https://eywep.cdjscedeao.org/fr/ressources/13/telecharger

The post ECOWAS Youth Entrepreneurship Programme (EYEP) 2026: Win Up to $15,000 in Grants for Young Entrepreneurs Across Seven West African Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: 4 Major Grant Opportunities for NGOs, Civil Society Organizations, Social Enterprises, and Healthcare Organizations in 2026
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
Organizations working to create positive social, environmental, and health impact now have access to several outstanding funding opportunities designed to strengthen their programs, expand their reach, and improve sustainability. Whether you are a nonprofit, civil society organization, community-based organization (CBO), social enterprise, or healthcare institution, these grants provide financial resources, strategic partnerships, and capacity-building support […]

The post 4 Major Grant Opportunities for NGOs, Civil Society Organizations, Social Enterprises, and Healthcare Organizations in 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Organizations working to create positive social, environmental, and health impact now have access to several outstanding funding opportunities designed to strengthen their programs, expand their reach, and improve sustainability. Whether you are a nonprofit, civil society organization, community-based organization (CBO), social enterprise, or healthcare institution, these grants provide financial resources, strategic partnerships, and capacity-building support to help maximize your impact.

This article highlights four major grant opportunities currently available for organizations across Africa, Sub-Saharan Africa, low- and middle-income countries, and other eligible regions. These opportunities are offered by respected international organizations committed to supporting locally led development, social innovation, environmental protection, and healthcare quality improvement.

The funding opportunities featured include:

  • Oak Foundation Multi-Year Grants
  • New Africa Fund Matching Grants
  • Segal Family Foundation Partnership Funding
  • ISQua Quality Improvement Grants

Below is everything you need to know about each opportunity.

 

1. Oak Foundation Multi-Year Grants (Rolling Applications)

The Oak Foundation provides substantial multi-year grants to nonprofit organizations and civil society organizations working to achieve long-term social and environmental change.

Unlike many grant programs, organizations do not necessarily need to receive an invitation before expressing their interest. Eligible organizations whose work aligns closely with the Foundation’s priorities may submit an unsolicited Letter of Inquiry (LOI) for consideration.

Oak Foundation is known for supporting initiatives that create sustainable, systemic, and long-lasting impact rather than short-term interventions.

Funding Available

Organizations may receive:

  • Average grant size of approximately USD $900,000
  • Funding awards of up to USD $10 million for exceptional initiatives

The Foundation primarily supports organizations capable of delivering significant impact in areas aligned with its funding priorities.

Who Can Apply?

Eligible applicants include:

  • Nonprofit organizations
  • Civil society organizations

Applicants should demonstrate strong alignment with the Oak Foundation’s funding priorities.

Deadline

Rolling applications (No fixed deadline)

Organizations may submit a Letter of Inquiry throughout the year.

 

2. New Africa Fund Matching Grants 

The New Africa Fund is supporting early-stage organizations across Africa by providing financial assistance together with organizational development support.

The initiative aims to strengthen the long-term sustainability of African organizations by combining matching grants with fiscal sponsorship and capacity-building opportunities.

Funding Available

Successful organizations may receive:

  • Up to USD $5,000 in matching funds

Additional Benefits

Selected organizations will also benefit from:

  • Twelve (12) months of free fiscal sponsorship
  • Capacity-building opportunities
  • Access to organizational development support
  • Opportunity for top-performing organizations to attend an intensive workshop in Kigali, Rwanda

These additional services are designed to help organizations strengthen governance, fundraising capacity, and operational sustainability.

Eligible Applicants

Applications are welcomed from:

  • Non-governmental organizations (NGOs)
  • Community-Based Organizations (CBOs)
  • Social enterprises

Organizations must be located within one of the 55 African Union Member States.

Deadline

14 August 2026

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline.

 

3. Segal Family Foundation Partnership Funding

The Segal Family Foundation supports locally led organizations that are driving sustainable development across Sub-Saharan Africa.

Rather than operating through a traditional open grant competition, the Foundation builds long-term partnerships with organizations demonstrating exceptional impact and leadership.

Its funding approach emphasizes trust-based philanthropy by providing flexible, unrestricted support that allows organizations to invest where resources are most needed.

Funding Support

Partner organizations receive:

  • Flexible unrestricted funding
  • Long-term strategic partnership
  • Organizational strengthening support
  • Capacity development
  • Relationship-based collaboration

Because the funding is unrestricted, organizations have greater flexibility to allocate resources according to their strategic priorities.

Who Can Apply?

The Foundation works with:

  • High-impact organizations
  • Locally led development organizations
  • Organizations operating across Sub-Saharan Africa

Application Process

There is no open call for applications.

Instead, new partners are identified through trusted referral networks and strategic recommendations.

Organizations interested in learning more can review the Foundation’s partnership approach through its official website.

 

4. ISQua Quality Improvement Grant 

The International Society for Quality in Health Care (ISQua) is inviting applications from organizations committed to improving healthcare quality and patient safety in low- and middle-income countries.

The grant supports initiatives that strengthen healthcare systems and improve the delivery of safe, high-quality healthcare services.

Funding Available

Successful applicants may receive:

  • €15,000

Eligible Organizations

The grant is open to:

  • Healthcare organizations
  • Hospitals
  • Professional associations
  • Health-focused NGOs

Applicants should be based in low- and middle-income countries (LMICs).

Deadline

12 August 2026

Interested organizations should complete their applications before the closing date.

 

Comparison of the Four Funding Opportunities

Opportunity Funding Eligible Applicants Deadline
Oak Foundation Average USD $900,000 (up to USD $10 million) Nonprofits and civil society organizations Rolling
New Africa Fund Up to USD $5,000 + fiscal sponsorship NGOs, CBOs, Social Enterprises in Africa 14 August 2026
Segal Family Foundation Flexible unrestricted funding High-impact organizations in Sub-Saharan Africa No open call
ISQua €15,000 Healthcare organizations and health NGOs in LMICs 12 August 2026

 

Why Organizations Should Consider These Opportunities

Each of these funding opportunities supports organizations seeking to increase their impact while strengthening institutional capacity.

Depending on your organization’s mission, these opportunities can provide:

  • Significant grant funding
  • Flexible unrestricted financial support
  • Long-term strategic partnerships
  • Capacity-building opportunities
  • Fiscal sponsorship
  • Professional networking
  • Healthcare quality improvement funding
  • Organizational sustainability support
  • Access to international development partners

Whether your organization focuses on education, health, community development, environmental protection, social innovation, or entrepreneurship, one of these opportunities may align with your goals.

Organizations are encouraged to carefully review the eligibility requirements and application procedures for each fund and prepare strong proposals well in advance of the applicable deadlines.

 

Apply here 

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Grant opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post 4 Major Grant Opportunities for NGOs, Civil Society Organizations, Social Enterprises, and Healthcare Organizations in 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
